reforms.description: The Polish government aims to enhance the funding landscape for public higher education institutions by allowing them to charge cost-related fees while increasing grants to maintain accessibility. This initiative is part of a broader program to boost R&D expenditures to 1.5% of GDP by 2006, addressing funding inequities and improving resource allocation in the education sector.
reforms.package_name: Higher Education and R&D Funding Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing funding for higher education and R&D is expected to enhance the quality of education and research output, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program aimed at increasing R&D expenditures was adopted and implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in funding for higher education and R&D.
reforms.source_quote: In 2000 the government adopted a programme entitled 'Increasing Innovativeness of the Polish Economy to 2006,' and the National Development Plan for 2004-06 targeted an increase in R&D spending to 1.5% of GDP by 2006.

reforms.description: The 2005 law on support for innovation aims to enhance the flow of finance to innovative companies by leveraging private finance and involving venture capital. It establishes a fund managed by a state-owned bank to lend up to 75% of project costs for innovative projects and extends tax concessions for innovation expenditures to incentivize firms to invest in technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to finance and incentivizing innovation investments, this reform is expected to stimulate economic growth and enhance competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was passed and is actively supporting innovation financing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a major overhaul of the innovation financing landscape in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: Other parts of the 2005 law to support innovation consist of extending various tax concessions to innovation expenditure, mainly allowing accelerated or 100% depreciation for technology investment.

reforms.description: The establishment of the Polish Agency for Enterprise Development (PARP) aims to manage funds for entrepreneurship, providing grants for business advisory services, training, and micro-credit to support innovation and entrepreneurship in Poland.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted support for entrepreneurship, this reform is expected to foster innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: PARP has been established and is operational in supporting entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of PARP represents a significant institutional change aimed at enhancing support for entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The main institution supplying active support for entrepreneurs is the Polish Agency for Enterprise Development (PARP), set up in 2000.
